title: The Crows of Agra
author: Sharath Komarraju
synopsis: The first novel in the Birbal series, set in 1562 A.D. when Akbar is just twenty. After his regent Bairam Khan is found murdered, Akbar must uncover the killer with the help of Mahesh Das, a clever Brahmin who later becomes the legendary Birbal. A historical whodunit populated by figures of the Mughal court, it casts Birbal as a sharp-witted detective solving a palace murder.

title: The Seal of Surya: The Legend of Ikshvaku
author: Amritanshu Pandey
synopsis: The first book of The Scrolls of Aryavarta series tells the story of Ikshvaku, the boy king and first Manava, founder of the first unified kingdom in the Indian subcontinent. Set after the age of Devas and Asuras, the solar tribes are left without a ruler and the Seal of Aurvan Surya is lost. Ikshvaku saves the solar tribes, becomes king of Aryavarta, and embarks on a lifelong war and search for the lost seal across rugged terrain. A tale of betrayal, loyalty, dynasties and destruction.

title: The Murder of Roger Ackroyd
author: Agatha Christie
synopsis: The fourth Hercule Poirot novel, published in 1926. When wealthy Roger Ackroyd is found stabbed in his study, the recently retired Poirot is drawn out of his quiet village life to investigate. With a confounding murder, a small list of suspects, and clues and red herrings throughout, the story builds to one of the most famous and startling twist endings in detective fiction.

title: Chariots of the Gods
author: Erich von Däniken
synopsis: First published in 1968, this world-famous bestseller introduced the theory that ancient Earth was visited by extraterrestrials welcomed as gods, who gave early civilizations their technologies and religions. Von Daniken bases his startling claims on studies of ancient ruins, lost cities, potential spaceports, and other evidence he argues points to extraterrestrial intervention in human history. The book inspired countless books and films and remains a landmark of the ancient-astronaut genre.

title: Three mistakes of my life
author: Chetan Bhagat
synopsis: Chetan Bhagat's third novel, set in Ahmedabad, follows three friends: Govind, who dreams of business success; Ish, devoted to nurturing a gifted young cricketer named Ali; and Omi, who just wants to stay with his friends. Together they open a cricket shop, but nothing comes easy in a turbulent city. To reach their goals they must face religious politics, an earthquake, riots, forbidden love, and above all their own mistakes.

title: autobiography of a yogi
author: Paramahansa Yogananda
synopsis: A modern spiritual classic first published in 1946, this is at once a beautifully written account of an exceptional life and a profound introduction to the ancient science of Yoga and meditation. Yogananda recounts his remarkable childhood, his search across India for an illumined teacher, years of training under a revered master, and the decades he spent teaching in America. It records his meetings with figures such as Mahatma Gandhi, Rabindranath Tagore, and Luther Burbank.

title: The collected works of Khalil Ghibran
author: Khalil Gibran
synopsis: An omnibus gathering the major works of the beloved Lebanese poet, artist and mystic Kahlil Gibran into one comprehensive volume. Included are The Prophet, The Wanderer, Jesus the Son of Man, A Tear and a Smile, Spirits Rebellious, Nymphs of the Valley, prose poems and other works. Gibran's essays, stories, parables and prose poems are imbued with a powerful simplicity and wisdom, addressing marriage, children, friendship, grief, work and pleasure.

title: Rumi's Little book of Life
author: Rumi / Translated by Kabir Helminski
synopsis: A collection of 196 poems by Rumi, previously unavailable in English, translated by native Persian speakers Maryam Mafi and Azima Melita Kolin. This mystical poetry focuses on the inner life and serves as a guidebook for the inner journey. Through an ascending hierarchy it restores the soul to the heart, the heart to the spirit, and in finding spirit transcends the everyday world of dust.

title: The Diary of a Young Girl - Anne Frank
author: Anne Frank
synopsis: The world-famous diary of Anne Frank, documenting her life in hiding from 1942 to 1944. Given a journal on her thirteenth birthday, Anne filled it with her thoughts, fears, feelings, and day-to-day life while hiding with her family from the Nazis in 1940s Amsterdam. First published in 1947 as Het Achterhuis and translated into over seventy languages, it is one of the world's best-known books.

title: A Room of Ones Own - Virginia Woolf
author: Virginia Woolf
synopsis: An extended essay by Virginia Woolf, first published in 1929 and based on lectures she delivered at two women's colleges at Cambridge. Using a fictional narrator to explore women as both writers and characters in fiction, it is a landmark feminist text arguing for a literal and figurative space for women writers within a literary tradition dominated by patriarchy. It is famous for its dictum that a woman must have money and a room of her own if she is to write fiction.

title: Shadow Play - Shashi Deshpande
author: Shashi Deshpande
synopsis: A sequel to A Matter of Time, published in 2013, this novel is a meditation on kinship, marriage, ambition and the changing face of urban India. Picking up ten years later, it returns to Aru and her sisters, left bereft after their mother's death and father's desertion. Aru navigates married life and life's unpredictability, still uncertain about reaching out and being vulnerable, while the story confronts family tragedy, violence, and the cruelties inflicted within families.

title: That Long Silence - Shashi Deshpande
author: Shashi Deshpande
synopsis: Jaya, a failed writer, sees her familiar existence disrupted when her husband's reputation is questioned and the family's future is thrown into jeopardy. Forced to confront the frustrations of her seventeen-year marriage, her disappointment in her two teenage children, and the claustrophobia of her childhood, she reflects on the silences that have shaped her life. Widely regarded as a feminist text of immense power, the novel won the Sahitya Akademi Award.

title: The Night of Broken Glass
author: Feroz Rather
synopsis: The Night of Broken Glass is a collection of thirteen interconnected short stories in which the same set of characters move in and out, sometimes central to the narrative and at other times witnesses in the background. Set against three decades of insurgency in Kashmir, the stories render in fiction the effects of violence on the region's inhabitants. Through this tapestry, Feroz Rather weaves the horror that Kashmir has come to represent.

title: Selected Stories of Charles Dickens
author: Charles Dickens
synopsis: Charles Dickens is known not only for his novels but also for his short stories, particularly A Christmas Carol. This selection gathers some of his shorter works, including pieces such as The Haunted Man written for the Christmas market, all laying emphasis on family love and the delights of home. The collection showcases Dickens's distinctive wit and imaginative power in the shorter narrative form.

title: A Matter of Time - Shashi Deshpande
author: Shashi Deshpande
synopsis: Set in present-day Karnataka, A Matter of Time explores the relationships within an extended family across three generations. When Gopal walks out on his wife, Sumi, and their three daughters, Sumi returns with them to the Big House where her parents have lived in oppressive silence, not speaking to each other for thirty-five years. As the mystery of that long silence is unraveled, a story of loss and pain is laid bare, one that seems to be repeating itself in Sumi's own life.

title: The Psychology of Money
author: Morgan Housel
synopsis: In The Psychology of Money, Morgan Housel shares 19 short stories exploring the different ways people think about money, teaching readers to make better sense of one of life's most important topics. Doing well with money isn't necessarily about what you know, it's about how you behave. In the real world people don't make financial decisions on a spreadsheet; they make them at the dinner table where personal history, ego, pride, and odd incentives are all scrambled together.

title: Attitude is everything
author: Jeff Keller
synopsis: Jeff Keller shows how the road to a happier, more successful life starts with your attitude. Drawing on his own career transition from lawyer to motivational speaker, he demonstrates how to take control and unleash hidden potential through three decisive steps: think, speak, and act. The book is divided into three parts covering how success begins in the mind, watching your words, and taking action.

title: The rudest book ever
author: Shwetabh Gangwar
synopsis: The Rudest Book Ever is a straight-talking, no-nonsense self-help guide that helps readers recognize the ignorance they hold toward themselves and free their minds from everyday nonsense. Author and problem-solver Shwetabh Gangwar offers practical principles and perspectives for dealing with the difficulties that have happened, are happening, and will happen to you. It is a refreshing, easy-to-read guide that will make you rethink much of what you've been taught.

title: The secret
author: Rhonda Bryne
synopsis: The Secret centers on the law of attraction, the belief that your thoughts shape your reality. Drawing on wisdom said to have been used by great figures throughout history, Rhonda Byrne reveals how to apply this knowledge in every aspect of life. Readers learn how to use The Secret for money, health, relationships, happiness, and every interaction they have in the world.

title: The 7 habits of highly effective people
author: Stephen R. Covey, Jim Collins
synopsis: Stephen R. Covey presents a holistic, integrated, principle-centered approach for solving personal and professional problems. With penetrating insights and pointed anecdotes, he reveals a step-by-step pathway for living with fairness, integrity, honesty, and human dignity. The seven habits move a person progressively from dependence to independence to interdependence, offering timeless lessons in personal change.

title: What every Indian should know before investing
author: Vinod Pottayil
synopsis: This book explains the majority of investment options available to Indian investors, from Fixed Deposits, PPF, and Real Estate to Gold, Mutual Funds, and Stocks, covering the advantages, disadvantages, and tax implications of each. It also includes comprehensive chapters on Life Insurance, Health Insurance, the National Pension Scheme, writing a will, and making a financial plan. Written in simple English, it is aimed at people of all ages who want to take control of their financial lives.

title: Circe
author: Madeline Miller
synopsis: In the house of Helios, god of the sun, a daughter is born. But Circe is a strange child, neither powerful like her father nor viciously alluring like her mother, and she turns to the world of mortals for companionship, discovering that she possesses the power of witchcraft. Threatened, Zeus banishes her to a deserted island, where she hones her occult craft and crosses paths with many of the most famous figures in Greek mythology. Yet a woman who stands alone will eventually draw the wrath of both gods and men.

title: The Namesake
author: Jhumpa Lahiri
synopsis: The Namesake takes the Ganguli family from their tradition-bound life in Calcutta through their fraught transformation into Americans. On the heels of their arranged wedding, Ashoke and Ashima Ganguli settle in Cambridge, Massachusetts, where their son Gogol, named after the celebrated Russian author, grows up rejecting both his unusual name and his Bengali heritage. Lahiri explores the immigrant experience, the clash of cultures, and the tangled ties between generations.

title: Gone Girl
author: Gillian Flynn
synopsis: On a warm summer morning in North Carthage, Missouri, it is Nick and Amy Dunne's fifth wedding anniversary when Amy suddenly disappears. Under mounting pressure from the police and a growing media frenzy, Nick's portrait of the perfect couple begins to crack. Told through alternating perspectives and Amy's diary entries, the novel is a dark exploration of marriage, deception, and unreliable narration that keeps readers guessing until the end.

title: Digital Fortress
author: Dan Brown
synopsis: When the NSA's invincible code-breaking machine encounters a mysterious code it cannot break, the agency calls its head cryptographer, Susan Fletcher, a brilliant and beautiful mathematician. What she uncovers sends shock waves through the corridors of power. The NSA is being held hostage, not by guns or bombs, but by a code so ingeniously complex that if released it would cripple U.S. intelligence.

title: Sapiens
author: Yuval Noah Harari
synopsis: Sapiens explores the ways in which biology and history have defined us and enhanced our understanding of what it means to be human. From examining the role evolving humans have played in the global ecosystem to charting the rise of empires, Harari integrates history and science to reconsider accepted narratives, connect past developments with contemporary concerns, and examine specific events within the context of larger ideas.

title: Shoe Dog
author: Phil Knight
synopsis: In this candid and riveting memoir, Nike founder Phil Knight shares the inside story of the company's early days as a start-up and its evolution into one of the world's most iconic, profitable, and controversial brands. Beginning in 1962 with a crazy idea to import running shoes from Japan, Knight recounts the risks, setbacks, rivals, and triumphs that shaped Nike, offering an honest look at the messy, myth-shattering reality of building something new.

title: Jack Reacher - Killing Floor
author: Lee Child
synopsis: Jack Reacher jumps off a bus and walks fourteen miles down a country road into Margrave, Georgia. He is the only stranger in town on the day they have their first homicide in thirty years, and the police arrest him, with the chief of police turning eyewitness to place him at the scene. Introducing Reacher as a tough ex-military cop of no fixed abode, this debut launches one of the most popular action-thriller series in fiction.

title: Defending Jacob
author: William Landay
synopsis: Assistant District Attorney Andy Barber is called to a gruesome crime scene after fourteen-year-old Ben Rifkin is found brutally stabbed in a local park. Suspicion turns quickly to Andy's own son, Jacob, one of Ben's classmates, and the Barber family is thrust into a nightmare. A suspenseful, character-driven tale of guilt, betrayal, and the terrifying speed at which our lives can spin out of control.

title: A thing beyond forever
author: Novoneel Chakraborty
synopsis: Dr. Radhika Sharma appears to the world as an arrogant feminist, but inside she is a broken person still coming to terms with the sudden death of her first love, Raen. A nine-year-old patient under her treatment is not only infatuated with her but keeps asking her non-stop questions, one of which leads her to open Raen's personal diary. As she reads, Radhika discovers an uncanny similarity between Raen and the young boy in this soul-stirring love story.

title: Sivagami Sabhatham -1 & 2 - Kalki
author: Kalki Krishnamurthy
synopsis: Set in seventh-century South India, this historical Tamil epic centers on the struggle for supremacy between the Chalukya Emperor Pulikesi II and the Pallava rulers Mahendra Varmar and his son Narasimha Varmar. Woven through the political and military conflict is the ardent yet ill-fated romance between Crown Prince Narasimha Varmar and Sivakami, the beautiful and talented dancer who is the daughter of the land's foremost sculptor, Aayanar. It is regarded as one of the greatest novels ever written in Tamil.

title: One night at the call center
author: Chetan bhagat
synopsis: The story follows six call center employees working the night shift at a call center in Gurgaon, India, each grappling with their own anxieties around career, love, marriage, and family. Over the course of a single eventful night, cultural cross-wires, workplace pressures, and personal crises collide. Everything changes when the group receives a mysterious phone call from God.

title: Angels & Demon
author: Dan Brown
synopsis: Harvard symbologist Robert Langdon is summoned after a physicist is found murdered and a canister of deadly antimatter goes missing, hidden somewhere in Vatican City and set to explode within twenty-four hours. Langdon joins forces with scientist Vittoria Vetra in a frantic race across Rome, confronting the resurgence of the ancient secret society known as the Illuminati. The threat targets the Vatican and the cardinals gathered to elect the next Pope.

title: The Power of your Subconscious Mind
author: Joseph Murphy
synopsis: A bestseller since its first publication in 1963, this classic self-help work argues that the events of your life are the result of the interplay between your conscious and subconscious minds. Dr. Joseph Murphy explains practical techniques for focusing and redirecting this inner energy to change your destiny. He shows how readers can heal themselves, banish fears, sleep better, improve relationships, and feel happier.

title: The Monogram Murders
author: Sophie Hannah
synopsis: The first Hercule Poirot mystery sanctioned by the Agatha Christie estate. At London's Bloxham Hotel, three guests are found murdered in separate rooms, each posed with an identical monogrammed cufflink placed in the mouth. Shortly before, Poirot encounters a terrified woman named Jennie in a coffee house who insists she is about to be murdered and that she will have deserved it. Poirot must unravel how the cases connect before the killer strikes again.

title: The Mystery of Three Quarters
author: Sophie Hannah
synopsis: The third new Hercule Poirot mystery by Sophie Hannah. Poirot is pressed into service when multiple people arrive at his door, furious over letters he supposedly sent accusing each of them of murdering a man named Barnabas Pandy. Poirot has never heard of Pandy and certainly sent no such letters, and it isn't even clear a crime has been committed. He must untangle who forged his name and why.

title: The Double Clue
author: Agatha Christie
synopsis: A collection of Hercule Poirot short stories. In the title story, only four people had the opportunity to steal Marcus Hardman's medieval jewels, and when Poirot examines the scene of the crime he finds two clues that lead to the culprit. It also marks Poirot's encounter with Countess Vera Rossakoff, his only acknowledged love interest.

title: By the Pricking of My Thumbs
author: Agatha Christie
synopsis: Featuring Christie's detectives Tommy and Tuppence Beresford, now middle-aged. While visiting an elderly aunt at a gothic British nursing home, the pair encounter hints of poisoning and mysterious secrets. They investigate strange and troubling doings behind the scenes, uncovering a sinister mystery hidden beneath the home's respectable surface.

title: The 5th Horseman
author: James Patterson
synopsis: The fifth Women's Murder Club thriller by James Patterson and Maxine Paetro. Lieutenant Lindsay Boxer and attorney Yuki Castellano lead an investigation into a string of mysterious patient deaths at San Francisco Municipal Hospital, whose administration is determined to shield its reputation at all costs. As an explosive court battle grips the nation, the Women's Murder Club hunts a merciless killer among the medical staff.

title: Crooked House
author: Sophie Hannah
synopsis: The Leonides are one big happy family living in a sprawling, ramshackle mansion, until the head of the household, Aristide, is murdered with a fatal barbiturate injection. Suspicion naturally falls on the old man's young widow, fifty years his junior. But the murderer has reckoned without the tenacity of Charles Hayward, fiance of the late millionaire's granddaughter. One of Christie's own favourites, with a famously shocking ending.

title: The Girl You Left Behind
author: Jojo Moyes
synopsis: A dual-timeline novel. In occupied France in 1916, Sophie is left behind when her artist husband goes to fight, and a German Kommandant becomes dangerously fixated on her and the portrait her husband painted of her. In present-day London, grieving widow Liv Halston owns that same painting and, with art investigator Paul, becomes caught up in the mystery of Sophie's fate and the painting's contested history.

title: Thanks for the Memories
author: Cecelia Ahern
synopsis: After a devastating miscarriage and the collapse of her marriage, Joyce Conway moves in with her eccentric father and begins recalling memories that are not her own. They belong to Justin Hitchcock, a divorced architecture lecturer in Dublin who recently gave blood at a university blood drive. A touch of magical realism ties their lives together in this warm, unusual love story.

title: A Stranger in the House
author: Shari Lapena
synopsis: Karen and Tom Krupp seem happily settled in upstate New York until Tom comes home to find Karen gone, having left in a rush without her purse or phone. She has been in a car accident in the worst part of town and claims she can't remember what she was doing there. The police suspect her convenient memory loss, and when Karen senses someone has been in her house, the questions only multiply.

title: Hollywood Bliss My life so far
author: Chloe Rayban
synopsis: The second book in the Hollywood Bliss series. Hollywood Bliss Winterman and her mega-famous popstar mother Kandhi are moving to New York City, and as usual things are anything but blissful. An army of stylists and assistants flutter around her mum, her father has a severe case of hypochondria, and her annoying soon-to-be-stepbrother Shug is always lurking. All Holly wants is to be an ordinary girl.

title: NYPD RED 5
author: James Patterson
synopsis: Published as Red Alert, this is the fifth NYPD Red novel by James Patterson and Marshall Karp. Detectives Zach Jordan and Kylie MacDonald of the elite NYPD Red task force respond after a bomb goes off while they provide security to the Mayor. A tale of revenge, greed, blackmail and jealousy, the investigation draws them into underground drug cartels and corruption at the highest levels of society.

title: Ghachar Ghochar
author: Vivek Shanbhag
synopsis: Translated from the Kannada by Srinath Perur, this slim novel follows a close-knit middle-class family in Bangalore whose lives are upended when a spice business brings sudden wealth. Narrated by an unnamed young man who idles away his days at a coffee house, it traces how money quietly corrodes the family's bonds and values, turning everything into ghachar ghochar, a tangled, inextricable mess.

title: Heads You Win
author: Jeffery Archer
synopsis: In 1968 Leningrad, Alexander Karpenko and his mother must flee Russia after his father is assassinated by the KGB. At the docks they face an irreversible choice determined by the toss of a coin: board a ship for America or for Great Britain. The novel follows both paths, tracing Alex in New York and Sasha in London across thirty years of triumphs and defeats, building to a final twist.

title: And Thereby Hangs a Tale
author: Jeffery Archer
synopsis: A collection of fifteen contemporary short stories by Jeffrey Archer, ten of them inspired by real events gathered from around the globe. Tales include a couple who fall in love waiting for a traffic light in Delhi, a priceless family oil painting, and a life changed by a golf ball in a Christmas cracker. The title is drawn from Shakespeare's The Taming of the Shrew.

title: The Sins of the Father
author: Jeffery Archer
synopsis: Picking up where Only Time Will Tell ends, Harry Clifton is arrested after assuming the name Tom Bradshaw and accused of murdering his brother. Only days before Britain declares war on Germany, Harry, hoping to escape long-buried family secrets and forced to accept he may never marry Emma Barrington, joins the Merchant Navy, but his ship is sunk in the Atlantic by a German U-boat. The second book in The Clifton Chronicles.

title: Only Time will Tell
author: Jeffery Archer
synopsis: Beginning in 1920, this is the epic tale of Harry Clifton, a working-class boy from the docks of Bristol whose life is defined by the death of his father in the war. A remarkable gift wins him a scholarship to an exclusive boys' school and entry into a world he never imagined. Over twenty years, as the Second World War draws nearer, Harry learns the awful truth about his father's death and his ties to the powerful Barrington shipping family.

title: Honour Among Thieves
author: Jeffery Archer
synopsis: Set two years after the Gulf War in the early Clinton administration, Saddam Hussein hatches a cunning plan to humiliate the United States by stealing the Declaration of Independence and burning it on the Fourth of July. A crooked American lawyer is drawn into the scheme, while a Mossad agent is sent to Iraq, teaming up with an American law professor working as an agent.

title: The Night Crew
author: John Sanford
synopsis: Anna Batory runs the night crew, a small band of freelance video journalists who roam Los Angeles from dusk to dawn chasing accidents, robberies, murders, and demonstrations to sell footage to news stations. When one of her shoots turns deadly, Anna finds herself targeted by a killer and teams up with the father of a boy caught on her film. A standalone thriller from the author of the Prey series.

title: Dracula
author: Bram Stoker
synopsis: Told through journals, letters, and newspaper clippings, this Gothic horror classic follows solicitor Jonathan Harker's journey to Count Dracula's castle in Transylvania. After Harker returns, a series of bizarre incidents unfolds around Whitby, and a strange malady begins draining the life from young Lucy. A band of allies led by Professor Van Helsing unites to hunt the vampire and stop his spread of terror across England.

title: The Famous Five: Five on a Secret Trail
author: Enid Blyton
synopsis: The Famous Five go camping and pitch their tent near a ruined, seemingly abandoned cottage. When Anne hears strange noises at night, the others don't believe her, until they all see ghostly lights. Is the cottage really haunted, or is there another, more sinister explanation? The fifteenth book in Enid Blyton's Famous Five series.

title: Ramayan
author: Valmiki
synopsis: R.K. Narayan's shortened modern prose retelling of the great Indian epic, drawing inspiration from the work of the eleventh-century Tamil poet Kamban. It recreates the excitement and joy of the original story of Rama and Sita, beloved figures known to almost every individual in India. A master novelist makes one of the world's supreme storytelling masterpieces accessible to English readers.

title: Game of thrones (Full series)
author: George R.R. Martin
synopsis: In the Seven Kingdoms of Westeros, summers can span decades and winters last a lifetime, and the cold is returning to the land of the Starks. Amid plots and counter-plots, tragedy and betrayal, victory and terror, allies and enemies, the fate of the Stark family hangs in the balance as each side endeavors to win the deadliest of conflicts: the game of thrones. The first book in the A Song of Ice and Fire series.

title: Well Behaved women
author: Caroline Lamond
synopsis: A sweeping historical novel set in the Golden Age of Hollywood, based on the real life of silent movie star Alla Nazimova. Through a dual timeline, it follows Alla as she reinvents herself after a troubled upbringing, alongside Maybelle Crabtree, a young girl from Texas arriving in Hollywood for the first time. Both women carry secrets about their pasts and previous relationships.

title: A Time to Kill
author: John Grisham
synopsis: When Carl Lee Hailey guns down the men who raped his ten-year-old daughter, the people of Clanton see it as justified. But when word spreads that a black man has killed two white men, extremists descend on the town. Young lawyer Jake Brigance takes the case that could make or break his career, and in the maelstrom of Clanton, could also get him killed.

title: Bones are forever
author: Kathy Reichs
synopsis: Forensic anthropologist Temperance Brennan is called to a squalid Montreal apartment where she discovers the bodies of three hidden newborns. The trail of the missing mother, a woman with a string of aliases, leads Tempe from Edmonton to Yellowknife, a remote diamond-mining city in the Northwest Territories. Alongside detectives Ryan and Ollie Hasty, she uncovers a web of deceit tied to the diamond trade.

title: Wings of Fire - Autobiography of APJ Abdul Kalam
author: APJ Abdul Kalam
synopsis: Wings of Fire is the autobiography of A.P.J. Abdul Kalam, the aerospace scientist who led India's space and missile programs and later became President of India. He recounts his early life, struggles, and the fortitude and chance that shaped his career. The book traces his journey from a humble childhood in Rameswaram to the heights of Indian science and technology.

title: Shoba de- Speed post
author: Shobhaa De
synopsis: In Speedpost, Shobhaa De writes a series of candid letters to her six children, sharing advice and reflections on growing up. She addresses adolescent anxieties about love, sex, and friendship, along with religion, the challenges of responsible parenting, death, remembrance, and family traditions. The book offers touching, personal meditations on motherhood and values.

title: The Mill on the Floss
author: George Eliot
synopsis: This semi-autobiographical novel follows siblings Tom and Maggie Tulliver growing up at Dorlcote Mill on the River Floss in rural England. Their close but troubled bond is tested by family bankruptcy, forbidden relationships, and personal tragedy. Maggie, clever and passionate, repeatedly sacrifices her own desires for family loyalty, culminating in a tragic conclusion.

title: She broke it I didn't
author: Raksha Bharadia
synopsis: A light-hearted look at modern relationships told through humorous anecdotes reflecting the clash between men's and women's perspectives.

title: Revolution 2020 by Chetan Bhagat
author: Chetan Bhagat
synopsis: Set in Varanasi, Revolution 2020 follows childhood friends Gopal, Raghav, and Aarti as they chase success and love in a society that rewards the corrupt. Gopal wants wealth, Raghav wants to change the system, and both love Aarti. The story becomes a love triangle intertwined with corruption, ambition, and a journey of self-discovery, exposing how coaching institutions exploit engineering aspirants.

title: The Leader who had no title by Robin Sharma
author: Robin Sharma
synopsis: Written as a modern business fable, the book follows Blake, who learns leadership through conversations with four unconventional mentors. Each encounter reveals principles showing that anyone, regardless of position or title, can lead. Robin Sharma argues that organizations thrive by fostering leadership at every level, with success rooted in taking responsibility, inspiring others, and leading through action.

title: Tamarind City
author: Bishwanath Ghosh
synopsis: Tamarind City is Bishwanath Ghosh's portrait of Chennai (formerly Madras), the city where modern India began. Wearing a reporter's cap, Ghosh explores its history, historic neighborhoods, and diverse residents, from a top vocalist to a sexologist, from a yoga teacher to a yesteryear film star. He shows how modern Indian institutions trace their roots to Fort St George, blending the city's colonial past with its contemporary life.

title: Sidney Sheldon - Tell me your dreams
author: Sidney Sheldon
synopsis: Three beautiful young women are suspected in a series of brutal murders, leading to an arrest and one of the most bizarre murder trials of the century. Based on actual events, the novel races from London to Rome to Quebec to San Francisco. At its heart is Ashley Patterson, whose past holds the key to a stunning psychological mystery involving multiple personality disorder.

title: Arthamulla Hindu madham - Kavingnar Kannadasan
author: Kavingar Kannadasan
synopsis: Arthamulla Indhu Matham (Meaningful Hindu Religion) is Kannadasan's most popular work, a multi-part series of essays on Hinduism. It offers an accessible elaboration of Hindu dharma, its religious practices, and the purpose behind various beliefs and rituals. The poet reflects on the meaning of Hindu religion as expressed through his own life and understanding.

title: Thaneer desam - Vairamuthu
author: Vairamuthu
synopsis: Thaneer Desam is a poetic Tamil novel by lyricist Vairamuthu, originally serialized in Ananda Vikatan in 1996. It follows Kalaivannan and Tamilroja, whose romance unfolds against the sea during a journey with fishermen meant to cure her water phobia. When their boat breaks down, a brief excursion becomes a survival ordeal, and the author weaves in scientific facts about oceans and marine life, all expressed in modern verse.

title: Harry Potter - Order of Phoenix
author: J.K. Rowling
synopsis: The fifth installment in the Harry Potter series. It's official: the evil Lord Voldemort has returned. As Harry returns to Hogwarts, the Ministry of Magic refuses to believe the truth, installing the cruel Dolores Umbridge as a teacher and undermining Dumbledore. Facing institutional powerlessness and a door that haunts his dreams, Harry finds strength through friendship, loyalty, and sacrifice, and a secret group known as the Order of the Phoenix gathers to fight the Dark forces.

title: The Almanack of Naval Ravikant
author: Eric Jorgenson
synopsis: A collection of Naval Ravikant's wisdom and experience curated from a decade of his most insightful interviews and reflections. Naval is an entrepreneur, philosopher, and investor known for his principles on building wealth and creating long-term happiness. Rather than a step-by-step guide, the book uses Naval's own words to help readers walk their own path toward a happier, wealthier life, covering wealth, happiness, decisions, learning, reading, and judgment.

title: The Art of Thinking Clearly
author: Rolf Dobelli
synopsis: In 99 short chapters, Rolf Dobelli reveals the most common errors of judgment and cognitive biases, and how to avoid them. Covering topics from the sunk cost fallacy to confirmation bias, the book offers simple, clear, and often surprising insights into how the mind works. It aims to transform decision-making at work, at home, and in everyday life, helping readers lead a more rational, less error-prone life.

title: The Culture Map
author: Erin Meyer
synopsis: Erin Meyer provides a field-tested model for decoding how cultural differences impact international business. The book focuses on eight scales where cultures diverge: how people communicate, evaluate, persuade, lead, decide, trust, disagree, and schedule. Combining an analytical framework with practical, actionable advice, it helps managers and professionals navigate the invisible boundaries of global business and work effectively across cultures.

title: A Wild Sheep Chase
author: Haruki Murakami
synopsis: A surreal and elaborate quest that takes readers from Tokyo to the remote mountains of northern Japan. An advertising executive casually uses a photograph sent by a friend, unaware it contains a mutant sheep with a star on its back. This unwittingly draws the attention of a menacing figure who demands he find the sheep or face dire consequences, sending the unnamed protagonist on a strange journey to confront his demons. It is the third book in the Trilogy of the Rat.

title: The Satvic Revolution
author: Subah Jain
synopsis: A practical guide to achieving peak health, increased energy, optimal weight, and a calm mindset amidst chaos. Written by Subah and Harshvardhan Saraf, co-founders of the Satvic Movement who healed themselves of chronic diseases through natural health wisdom, the book presents seven life-changing habits drawn from both ancient wisdom and modern science. The first four habits elevate physical health, while the last three address the often-overlooked mental and spiritual dimensions of wellbeing.

title: Guru Vivekanandar
author: S. Janakiraman
synopsis: A concise portrayal of Swami Vivekananda's life, teachings, and spiritual influence, highlighting how his ideas shaped modern Indian thought and youth inspiration.

title: Iyarkai velanmai
author: Nammalvar
synopsis: A practical guide to natural farming methods rooted in ecological balance, focusing on soil health, organic practices, and sustainable cultivation techniques for farmers.

title: Selva Dhaniyangal
author: S. Jayakumar
synopsis: Explores the nutritional value of traditional grains and millets, emphasizing their role in wholesome diets, disease prevention, and revival of native food habits.

title: Mupporul
author: Thirumuruga Kirubananda Variyar Swamigal
synopsis: A detailed explanation of the three core concepts in Tamil moral philosophy—God, Guru, and Self—presented through stories, commentary, and ethical teachings.

title: Marabu Kalvi
author: Suki Sivam
synopsis: Highlights traditional Tamil learning principles, drawing from ancient wisdom to discuss values, character formation, and holistic educational ideals.
